free_text: Measurement, evaluation and benchmarking of
differential cross sections for proton elastic
scattering on natO in the energy range E = 4-6 MeV,
suitable for EBS
The incident accelerator beam energy was corrected
with the the energy loss in the ultra thin surface
gold layer and the energy loss considering half of
the disodium phosphate target thickness.
Six Si surface barrier detectors, having a
thickness of 500 um.
Thin, multi-layered structure, consisting of thin
carbon foil, on top of which a layer of disodium
phosphate (Na2HPO4, 8-10 keV in thickness, for
Ep,lab = 6 to 4 MeV respectively), rich in oxygen,
was evaporated and, on top of this, an ultra-thin Au
layer (<1 keV) was also subsequently evaporated for
normalization and wear protection purposes.
Statistical uncertainty including the
statistical error in the integrated yield of the p-Au
scattering that was always kept below 2%, the
corresponding one of the p-natO scattering that did
not exceed 2-3% in the least favorable case and the
NAu/NnatO ratio of about 1.8%.
Systematic uncertainty originating from:
- accuracy of the implemented stopping power
- lateral inhomogeneities in the target composition
- carbon buildup effects
Data reveived from M.Kokkoris for IBANDL
VS
Fig. 2 of
Nucl.Instrum.Method Phys.Res.B 539(2023)15
